name: Ermita de la Virgen de las Vegas y Navafría — circular desde Pedraza
region_intro: Touring cycling around Pedraza features routes that navigate through varied terrain, including rolling hills and mountain passes. The region is characterized by its rural landscapes, often encompassing open fields and forested areas. Routes frequently involve significant elevation changes, indicative of the Sierra de Guadarrama foothills. The area provides a mix of challenging ascents and scenic descents suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Pedraza The most popular touring cycling route is Ermita de la Virgen de las Vegas y Navafría — circular desde Pedraza, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route offers a balanced ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Navafría Pass – Puerto de Navafría loop from Gallegos, a difficult 30.9 miles (49.8 km) path. This route features substantial climbs and descents through mountain pass scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Navafría Pass – Puerto de Navafría loop from Matabuena, a 48.1 miles (77.5 km) trail leading through mountain passes and challenging ascents, often completed in about 5 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Pedraza is defined by mountain passes, rolling hills, and rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult long-distance routes. The routes in Pedraza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 170 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Pedraza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Pedraza?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Pedraza, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The region is known for its varied terrain, including rolling hills and mountain passes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pedraza?
A: Yes, Pedraza offers a few easier routes. There are 2 routes classified as easy, and 11 as moderate, providing options for those new to touring cycling or looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes often navigate through rural landscapes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Pedraza?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107630495/ermita-de-la-virgen-de-las-vegas-y-navafria-circular-desde-pedraza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Virgen de las Vegas y Navafría — circular desde Pedraza&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 17.2 miles (27.7 km).

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Pedraza?
A: The terrain around Pedraza is quite varied, characterized by rolling hills and mountain passes, particularly in the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama. You'll encounter open fields, forested areas, and significant elevation changes, offering both challenging ascents and scenic descents.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region boasts several scenic spots. You can find impressive views at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Castillejos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6741653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejo Bajero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2347605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Linera&lt;/a&gt; also offer stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any waterfalls I can visit while cycling in Pedraza?
A: Yes, there are natural attractions including waterfalls. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3348944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mill and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4138646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho Litero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can add a refreshing stop to your ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Pedraza?
A: The touring cycling routes in Pedraza are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. Over 170 touring cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the varied terrain and scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Pedraza offers several challenging long-distance routes, with 23 routes classified as difficult. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40335340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass – Puerto de Navafría loop from Matabuena&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 48.1 miles (77.5 km) trail with significant ascents, typically taking around 5 hours 30 minutes to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Pedraza?
A: Given the presence of mountain passes and varied terrain, late spring, summer, and early autumn generally offer the most favorable conditions for touring cycling in Pedraza. During these seasons, the weather is typically more stable, and the mountain passes are clear.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through villages or offer places to explore?
A: Many routes traverse the rural landscapes and pass near charming settlements. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5784283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedraza&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, offering historical architecture and a chance to explore. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31447371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Matilla – San Pedro de Gaíllos loop from Colladillo&lt;/a&gt; will take you through the local countryside, connecting various points of interest.

Q: Are there routes that include mountain passes?
A: Yes, mountain passes are a defining feature of touring cycling around Pedraza. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass – Puerto de Navafría loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Linera – Navafría Pass loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for experiencing these challenging and scenic sections.
tips: It is located at the foot of the road that connects La Velilla and Val de San Pedro, close to the neighborhood of Requijada, which, together with other towns that do not exist today, made up what was known as the council of Las Vegas.

Navafría is a beautiful mountain town on the Segovian slope of the Sierra de Guadarrama that has restaurant services that are ideal for a stopover en route. It is popular among cyclists as well as the port of the same name that communicates with the Madrid slope.
Beautiful views of the city of Pedraza. It is worth adding this gazebo to any activity.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Vegas
